sources.notes: Statement and constraints: reconstruct any a with 0 <= a_i < 2^29 from all subsequence bitwise-AND sums modulo 1e9+7.
Used to confirm the per-bit count formula, high-to-low peeling, and prefix construction of one valid a.
Independent writeup used to confirm that only per-bit occurrence counts matter and that suffix-term peeling determines those counts.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution page; not used as evidence in this review.

annotation.summary: Given b_k equal to the sum of bitwise AND over all length-k subsequences of an unknown array a, reconstruct any valid a.
annotation.constraints: Sum n <= 1e5, a_i < 2^29, and a valid answer is guaranteed; O(n log A) with binomial precomputation is intended.
annotation.core_idea: For each bit independently, if cnt_l elements of a contain that bit, then this bit contributes 2^l * C(cnt_l,k) to b_k. Thus b is a sum of binomial-basis vectors indexed by bit counts. The current last coefficient reveals exactly which bits have cnt_l equal to the current suffix size, because the sum of possible bits is below the modulus and has no wrap ambiguity. Subtract their full contribution from all b_k, decrease the active size, and repeat. After all cnt_l are known, set bit l in the first cnt_l output positions to construct any valid array.
annotation.complexity: O(n log A) after O(n) factorial/inverse precomputation, with log A = 29.
tricks: bitwise AND decomposes by independent bits
binomial coefficient contribution by bit frequency
peel counts from largest subsequence size downward
construct arbitrary array from per-bit counts

solution_variants.name: bit-count-binomial-peeling
solution_variants.summary: Recover cnt_l for each bit by repeatedly reading the highest remaining b index, subtracting 2^l*C(m,k) contributions for bits with count m, then construct a by putting each recovered bit on a prefix of that length.

tags.evidence: The solution decomposes the AND sums into 29 independent bit contributions and reconstructs the answer bit by bit.
For each bit, its contribution to b_k is counted by the number of k-subsequences chosen entirely among cnt_l set positions, i.e. C(cnt_l,k).
Recovering the hidden bit counts is a triangular inversion of b_k = sum_l 2^l*C(cnt_l,k), peeled from large k to small k.
tags.description: Recover or manipulate quantities represented as linear combinations of binomial coefficient basis vectors C(m,k), especially via triangular high-to-low inversion.
tags.created_reason: Generic combinatorics does not distinguish this reusable binomial-basis inversion pattern from ordinary counting.

tags.evidence: After recovering only the per-bit counts, the algorithm explicitly builds any valid array by assigning each bit to the first cnt_l positions.
